name: celastrus paniculatus
mechanism: Preclinical studies describe antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, cholinergic, and neuroprotective signaling hypotheses.
effects: Review evidence highlights neuroprotective potential in experimental models.
Human clinical confirmation for neurological indications remains limited.
contraindications: Avoid use in pregnancy/breastfeeding until human safety is better characterized.
safetyNotes: Human dose-finding and long-term safety data are limited in modern controlled settings.
description: Celastrus paniculatus is an Ayurvedic medicinal seed plant often discussed for memory-related traditional use and modern neuroprotection research.
dosage: No validated, product-agnostic therapeutic dosing standard was identified in the promoted review source.

interactions: Potential CNS-active interactions are plausible but insufficiently quantified.
preparation: Traditional use frequently involves seed oil preparations with variable concentration.
region: Native to South and Southeast Asia, especially India and Sri Lanka.
traditionalUse: Traditional South Asian medicine describes seed oil use for memory and cognition support.
summary: Current promoted evidence for Celastrus paniculatus is mainly narrative and preclinical, supporting mechanistic plausibility but not definitive clinical efficacy.
